short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: Provides a short, intensive, hands-on introduction to the software and tools necessary for the MSc programmes in Computing Science: the Java and Python programming languages, associated tools, and the LINUX operating system.

requirements_of_entry.label: Requirements of Entry
value: Acceptance into one of the MSc programmes listed in section 10 below.

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value:
Weeks 1-6 of Semester 2 (six weeks)
Two hours of lectures and 5 hours of labs (two two-hour labs and one one-hour lab) per week
Mid-term coding test to be taken during one of the scheduled lab sessions.
Final coding test to be taken at the end of the semester

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value:
To enhance existing skills and practical processes for programming in Java and Python, which are the main languages used in the MSc programme
To improve skill at using online information to pick up new languages, APIs or tools
To become familiar with common tools needed to build working systems, including repositories, virtual machines and servers.
To develop knowledge of the use of the LINUX operating system and experience of using the tools available under LINUX for programming and system administration.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value:
Intermediate one-hour coding test, worth 15%, arranged in-house
Final two-hour coding test, worth 40%, arranged in-house.
Participation in team application exercises (tAPP) worth 5%
Three practical assessed exercises, worth 10%, 15%, 15% (total of 40%)

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value: Written feedback on assessed coursework; Verbal feedback during class discussions and laboratory sessions
